answer: Mathematics is recommended for most IT qualifications. Mathematical Literacy may limit admission to some programmes.
A diploma is usually shorter and more practical. A degree is generally longer and includes more theoretical and advanced study.
APS stands for Admission Point Score. It is calculated using your matric results and is used to determine eligibility for a programme.
The Bachelor of Information Technology is a 3-year degree focusing on software development, cloud computing, cybersecurity, and related IT fields.
The Bachelor of Computing is a 4-year degree that focuses on software engineering, data science, artificial intelligence, and advanced computing concepts.
A Higher Certificate is an entry-level qualification that provides foundational IT knowledge and can serve as a pathway to further studies.
The Diploma in Information Technology is a practical qualification that prepares students for careers in software development, infrastructure, business systems, and data analytics.
Cloud Computing involves designing, deploying, and managing applications and services using platforms such as AWS, Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud.
Cybersecurity focuses on protecting systems, networks, and data from cyber threats, attacks, and unauthorized access.
Software Development involves designing, coding, testing, and maintaining applications and software systems.
Data Science combines statistics, programming, and machine learning to analyze data and support decision-making.
Artificial Intelligence focuses on creating systems that can perform tasks requiring human intelligence, such as learning, reasoning, and problem-solving.
Internships provide practical workplace experience and help students develop industry-relevant skills before graduation.
The National Qualifications Framework (NQF) classifies qualifications in South Africa from Level 1 to Level 10 based on complexity and learning outcomes.
IT graduates can pursue careers such as Software Developer, Cloud Engineer, Data Analyst, Cybersecurity Specialist, Systems Administrator, and IT Consultant.
Database Development involves designing, creating, managing, and optimizing databases to store and retrieve information efficiently.
Common programming languages used in IT include Python, Java, C#, JavaScript, SQL, and C++.
Work Integrated Learning (WIL) combines academic studies with practical workplace experience to improve employability.
Most IT programmes require a National Senior Certificate and meeting the institution's APS and subject requirements.
Students may qualify for bursaries, scholarships, NSFAS funding, or institution-specific financial assistance depending on eligibility.
